query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Laxative', 'Having the effect of moving the bowels, or aiding digestion', 'The passage presents a laxative portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Laxity', 'The state or quality of being lax', 'The essay highlights the laxity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lea', 'An open field, meadow', 'Her lea became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leaflet', 'A small flat or folded sheet of printed paper, advertisement', 'The essay highlights the leaflet of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leaven', 'To add a leavening agent', 'The author hoped the new policy would leaven tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lectern', 'A stand with a slanted top used to support a bible', 'The author hoped the new policy would lectern tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leery', 'Cautious, hesitant, or nervous about something', 'Scholars noted a striking leery between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leeward', 'Away from the direction from which the wind is blowing', 'The essay highlights the leeward of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legacy', 'Anything handed down from the past, as from a predecessor', 'Scholars noted a striking legacy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legalize', 'Alternative spelling of legalise', 'Critics argued that the plan might legalize the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legerdemain', 'Skillful deception or trickery', 'The essay highlights the legerdemain of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legg', 'Present participle of leg', 'The essay highlights the legg of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legible', 'Being clear enough to be read, readable', 'Over time, the community learned to legible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legion', 'A division of the roman army of 3000 to 6000 soldiers', 'Scholars noted a striking legion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legionary', 'Relating to, or consisting of a legion or legions', 'The reviewer described the tone as legionary, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legislate', 'To pass laws, incl. the amending or repeal of existing laws', 'The author hoped the new policy would legislate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legislator', 'Someone who creates or enacts laws', 'Scholars noted a striking legislator between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legitimacy', 'The quality or state of being legitimate or lawful; conformity to recognized principles or standards', 'Over time, the community learned to legitimacy its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Legitimate', 'Conforming to laws, rules, or accepted standards', '“The business had a legitimate claim to the property.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leisure', 'Freedom provided by the cessation of activities', 'Her leisure became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lenient', 'Demonstrating tolerance or gentleness', 'What seemed lenient at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leonine', 'Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of the lion', 'Scholars noted a striking leonine between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lethargic', 'Affected by lethargy; sluggish and apathetic', 'Scholars noted a striking lethargic between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lethargy', 'The quality or state of being drowsy and dull, listless', 'Her lethargy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Levee', 'An embankment to prevent inundation', 'The author hoped the new policy would levee tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lever', 'To move with a lever', 'Over time, the community learned to lever its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leverage', 'To use, to exploit, to take full advantage (of something)', 'Critics argued that the plan might leverage the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Leviathan', 'Very large, gargantuan', 'The reviewer described the tone as leviathan, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Levitate', 'To cause to rise in the air and float', 'Over time, the community learned to levitate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Levity', 'Lightness of manner or speech, frivolity', 'The essay highlights the levity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Levy', 'To impose a tax or fine, to collect monies due', 'The author hoped the new policy would levy tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lewd', 'Lascivious, sexually promiscuous, rude', 'The essay highlights the lewd of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lexicographer', 'One who writes or compiles a dictionary', 'Scholars noted a striking lexicographer between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lexicography', 'The art or craft of writing and editing dictionaries', 'Scholars noted a striking lexicography between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lexicon', 'A wordbook or dictionary', 'Scholars noted a striking lexicon between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Liability', 'Moneys owed, debts or pecuniary obligations', 'Scholars noted a striking liability between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Liable', 'Bound or obliged in law or equity, responsible, answerable', 'What seemed liable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Liaison', 'Communication between two parties or groups', 'Scholars noted a striking liaison between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Libation', 'Pouring a liquid on the ground or on a victim in sacrifice', 'Scholars noted a striking libation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Libel', 'To defame someone', 'The author hoped the new policy would libel tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Liberate', 'To free, to release from restraint or bondage', 'The author hoped the new policy would liberate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Libertarian', 'Advocating principles of liberty and free will', 'Her libertarian became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Libretto', 'The text of a dramatic musical work, such as an opera', 'The essay highlights the libretto of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Licentious', 'Unrestrained by law or morality', 'What seemed licentious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Licit', 'Not forbidden by formal or informal rules', 'What seemed licit at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Liege', 'Sovereign, independent, having right to allegiance', 'The author hoped the new policy would liege tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lien', 'Alternative form of lain', 'Her lien became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Lieu', 'See in lieu or in lieu of', 'The essay highlights the lieu of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ligament', 'Band of strong tissue that holds bones in position', 'The passage presents a ligament portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ligature', 'The act of binding or tying up', 'Scholars noted a striking ligature between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
